item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, that certainly took an unexpected turn. I wonder if Cindy did ever exist.

There's a couple things here that might be typos.

The other humanoids hover overtop of the lake.

on top of the lake?

D-4314: We… we hung around on the pier about.

Not sure what the "about" is there for.

Well done mate, you did a wonderful job.

Here's to two hundred!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That-oooh… that last note really got me. I need a breather.
Is the gimmick that the D-class relived the night of the girl who drowned (getting pulled underwater)? Or is it something like that but different?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks!

I don't think it's as horrifying as other co76 entries but it's got a unique flair

Co76 horror has always been something I've found really interesting, yet really hard to replicate. It tends to walk a fine line between horror and pathos, generally straying into more the realm of horror by leaving more things unexplained. But here I guess we opted for a more comprehensible story, and that's why it feels a little different from other Co76 works. At least that's my guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am happy to say this is a spectacular ending compared to the previous drafts I looked at. Excellent use of nostalgia and the interactions were damn accurate +1. Sad my accidental self insert has less of a role now though ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, I don't think it's as horrifying as other co76 entries but it's got a unique flair with the smoke campers and it makes me wonder if SCP-3776 and this had any intramural scrimmages or whatever. I don't know how athletics works. But this works, so I'mma upvote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I still miss the title Summertime Fling, but then again The Water's Warm makes more sense. Then again, the water at the bottom of the lake probably isn't that warm.

This has greatly improved from the draft I looked at before— and it was already great then! That final note is pretty darn chilling. +1, I'm happy to see more Class of 76 stuff up on the wiki!

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
once again, i find myself digging kirby out of a hole, except this time the hole is an image licensing violation.

Header, public domain.
Map, also public domain (taken by the National Park Service)
Fire, CC.
All edited by me.

Thank you to everyone who looked at this — Nico, Ais, Roget, Jekeled, Kirby (duh), Crashington, Gecko, Ellie3, Tufto, Vezaz, NamelessMediocre, and likely several people on IRC who I forgot to note down, y'know, like a dumbass.

I hope you enjoy!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
What better to kick off the summer than some summer camp memories?

I'd like to thank Rounder for letting me jump on this draft with him so we can sink it in the middle of the eastern Atlantic.

I'd also like to thank everyone who looked this over, but I don't have that list of people so I'll just hope rounder puts that and the image credits in his post.
